companyAwards.note: Academy Scientific/Technical Award citations reconstructed from a third-party Oscar-nominations database and a German-language Wikipedia article, cross-checked against each other. Neither is the Academy's own site — treat these two dates as unverified until confirmed against oscars.org.

citations.citation: For the engineering and development of a high-speed re-recording system for motion picture production.
citations.type: Scientific/Technical Award (Academy Award for Technical Achievement)
citations.imageAlt: Academy Scientific and Technical Award trophy, presented to Magna-Tech Electronic Co.

citations.citation: For the introduction of the first remotely controlled Advance/Retard function for magnetic film sound dubbing.

companyAppreciation.note: A separate honor from the Scientific/Technical Awards above — recognition for supporting Academy facilities, not for an engineering achievement.

entries.citation: In appreciation of its generous support in providing services to the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences and, especially, to the Samuel Goldwyn Theater and Academy Little Theater. Its expertise has been of special value to members of the Academy and others who rely upon the facilities of the Academy for optimum quality of motion picture presentation.
entries.type: Certificate of Appreciation, presented by the Academy Board of Governors, July 2, 1991
entries.imageAlt: Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences certificate of appreciation to Magna-Tech Electronic Company, 1991

relatedIndividualAwards.note: This went to an individual associated with the company's early history, not to the company itself — keep the distinction clear on the page.

entries.citation: For the development of an Electronically Controlled Looping System.
entries.type: Academy Citation, Class III Scientific or Technical Award, December 31, 1969
entries.imageAlt: Academy Citation awarded to Otto Popelka of Magna-Tech Electronics Co., Inc., 1969

affiliateAwards.note: Recognitions to Continental Film & Digital Labs, an MTE-affiliated company — a different honor from the Academy awards above.

entries.recipient: Continental Film & Digital Laboratories, Inc. — Orlando
entries.citation: For participating in the Kodak Color Survey.
entries.imageAlt: Kodak Laboratory Quality Assurance certificate, Continental Film & Digital Laboratories Orlando, 2008

entries.recipient: Continental Film & Digital Laboratories, Inc. — Miami
entries.citation: For participating in the Kodak Monthly Process Survey.
entries.imageAlt: Kodak Laboratory Quality Assurance certificate, Continental Film & Digital Laboratories Miami, 2009

entries.recipient: Continental Film Labs of Florida Inc.
entries.citation: Thanks for your support of the ASC Cinematographers' Awards.
entries.type: Sponsor recognition plaque, American Society of Cinematographers
entries.imageAlt: ASC Cinematographers' Awards sponsor recognition plaque, Continental Film Labs of Florida

entries.recipient: Continental Film Labs of Florida Inc.
entries.citation: Thanks for your support of the 6th annual ASC Cinematographers' Awards.
entries.type: Sponsor recognition plaque, American Society of Cinematographers, 1992
entries.imageAlt: 6th annual ASC Cinematographers' Awards sponsor recognition plaque, Continental Film Labs of Florida, 1992
